beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and brew a pour-over coffee at home while checking the weather.
beats.reasoning: Kwame refuses drip coffee and loves his morning pour-over ritual. Grey rainy Tuesday ahead means he'll want that moment of control and quality before heading out. Moderate energy yesterday (50/100) means he's not rushing—taking time for the ritual.

beats.activity: Drive from Kirkland to SoDo garage, avoiding eastbound SR-520 closure by taking I-405 south.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound is closed 9am–noon; Kwame leaves early enough to avoid the crunch and takes the I-405 route instead. Rainy conditions add 10–15 min buffer. He's methodical and knows Seattle traffic patterns.

beats.activity: Work on vehicle diagnostics and repairs at the garage; steady rain outside keeps him focused indoors.
beats.reasoning: Rainy morning (7am–2pm steady) is ideal for focused garage work. Kwame is patient and chatty but also disciplined—he'll settle into diagnostic work and customer consultations. No urge to venture out in the drizzle.

beats.activity: Lunch at a nearby deli or food cart; grab something warm to eat while rain continues.
beats.location_name: Piroshky Piroshky (Pike Place Market, Downtown Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Rain is still steady at noon. Rather than a sit-down spot, Kwame grabs warm, hand-held food at Pike Place—a short walk from SoDo and quintessentially Seattle. Quick, satisfying, and he can chat with the vendor.

beats.activity: Walk back to the garage through light rain.
beats.reasoning: Short walk from Pike Place back to SoDo. Rain is tapering by 2pm, so it's manageable. Kwame doesn't mind a little weather and the walk clears his head between lunch and afternoon work.

beats.activity: Finish afternoon work: engine repair, customer consultations, and closing down the garage.
beats.reasoning: Rain clears by 2pm; Kwame stays focused on wrapping up the day's repairs. He's gregarious so he'll chat with colleagues and customers. Leaves at a reasonable time to avoid evening traffic and get back to Kirkland with daylight.

beats.activity: Drive from SoDo back to Kirkland via I-405 north and SR-520 (now reopened).
beats.reasoning: SR-520 closure ended at noon, so eastbound is clear by late afternoon. Still adding buffer for wet roads and typical evening traffic. Kwame prefers getting home before dark when possible.

beats.activity: Stop at Whole Foods to pick up supplies for dinner and a weekend brewing project.
beats.reasoning: On the way home or very close by. Kwame home-brews and will want quality ingredients. Quick, focused errand—he knows what he wants and doesn't linger. Moderate energy from yesterday means he's not depleted but not hyper-social.
Short final drive with groceries. Evening is settling in; Kwame wants to get home to prepare dinner and relax.

beats.activity: Cook dinner, eat, and prep gear for a possible rock climbing session or home-brewing work.; Work on home-brewing setup: clean equipment, prep ingredients, or test a batch.
beats.reasoning: Kwame values downtime after a steady work day. Rainy day means outdoor climbing is unlikely, so he settles into home-brewing prep or light tinkering. Modest energy, gregarious personality, but no big social plans tonight—just quality home time.

beats.activity: Wind down, read or relax, then sleep.
beats.reasoning: Solid 7 hours 45 minutes of sleep, ending just after 6:30am on Wednesday. Kwame had a steady, focused day—good work, reasonable self-care, and home time. He's ready for a full night's rest before tomorrow.

diary: Woke up to that familiar grey Seattle drizzle and made a solid pour-over before heading out. The SR-520 closure had everyone panicked, so I took I-405 south instead—dodged the worst of it. Work was steady all morning; there's something grounding about focusing on an engine while rain hammers the shop windows. Grabbed a piroshky from Pike Place on my lunch break, warm and perfect for the weather. The walk back through the light rain cleared my head a bit. Afternoon flew by with diagnostics and a couple of customer calls. By the time I hit the road home, the bridge was open again and traffic had settled. Stopped at Whole Foods for supplies, then spent the evening prepping my brewing setup. Got some new equipment to test out, and there's something satisfying about laying everything out clean and organized. Feels like the kind of day that needed slowing down—work, food, a little problem-solving at home. Ended it feeling productive and ready for the week ahead.
thought_process: The rain and bridge closure shaped everything today—I made sure to check the weather early and pivot my route without stress. Work felt meditative with the weather keeping me anchored indoors. I chose Pike Place for lunch because I needed something warm and grounding, a small ritual in the middle of the day. The walk back actually felt good despite the drizzle; I wasn't rushing. By afternoon, I was thinking about my brewing project—it's been on my mind, and I had enough daylight left to make real progress on the setup. I grabbed supplies at Whole Foods on purpose, treating it as part of my evening rather than a chore. The day had a natural rhythm: commute through chaos, focus at work, nourish myself, come home and build something. My mood stayed level throughout, maybe even lifted a bit once I got into the brewing prep. It's the kind of Tuesday that doesn't demand much but rewards attention.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
